zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：震荡偏弱整理
zh.content:
当前AVAX/USD短线维持震荡偏弱结构，价格运行于6.12附近，略低于20日均线6.189，表明多头动能不足，市场仍处整理阶段。

MACD数值为-0.121显示空头动能占优，短期下行动能仍存。RSI缺失使超买超卖判断有限，但整体动量偏向弱势震荡结构延续。

下方关键支撑位关注6.05与6.00整数关口，上方阻力集中在6.19及6.25区域，突破需成交量配合，否则易反复回落整理。

建议以区间震荡思路为主，短线可在6.05附近分批低吸，反弹至阻力区逐步止盈，严格设置止损控制波动风险注意仓位管理与分批执行。

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Avalanche (AVAX/USD) is currently trading near 6.13, posting a modest 1.08% intraday gain, yet price action remains constrained below the 20-period SMA at 6.189. The structure reflects a short-term consolidation phase following prior weakness, with buyers failing to sustain momentum above intraday highs. Overall trend leans neutral to mildly bearish, as repeated attempts to reclaim the SMA continue to be rejected.

Momentum indicators reinforce a cautious bias, with MACD reading at -0.12185099 signaling persistent bearish momentum, though the lack of a signal line reading limits crossover interpretation. Price trading below the SMA(20) at 6.18941 further confirms short-term weakness. Without RSI data, overbought/oversold conditions cannot be assessed, leaving momentum analysis skewed toward MACD and moving average structure, both currently favoring sellers bias.

AVAX/USD is currently oscillating within a tight intraday range between 6.0479 and 6.1336, indicating low volatility compression. Immediate support is located near 6.05, with a secondary psychological level at 6.00 likely to attract buyers on deeper pullbacks. On the upside, the 6.189 SMA(20) acts the first dynamic resistance, followed by stronger resistance around 6.25 where prior supply may re-emerge zone.

Trading conditions favor a range-bound strategy until a decisive breakout occurs. Short-term traders may consider buying near 6.05 support with tight risk control below 6.00, targeting mean reversion toward the SMA at 6.189. Alternatively, rejection near 6.19–6.25 resistance zone offers potential short setups. A sustained close above the SMA(20) would be required to shift bias toward bullish continuation and open upside momentum.
